notes.A (kimi / K2.7): had some issues; human ALSO detected it as the same model that was candidate-A in task-001 — blind cross-task style identification (both shuffles coincidentally dealt kimi the letter A)

agreement_vs_peer_consensus.difference: two adjacent swaps: human puts copilot 2nd / codex 3rd, and gpt-5.4 above kimi
agreement_vs_peer_consensus.trend: agreement dropped 0.90 (legible task) -> 0.80 (subjective task) — consistent with the subjectivity-loosens-grading hypothesis, but still high

verbosity_note: human ranking also tracks memo length (near-perfectly, one swap) — the length/quality confound is universal (models AND human), not a model bias; settle it with a length-capped task
